phases.name: Front-door summary + nav hub (walking skeleton + close confirmations) — GET /

steps.name: Wire the read-only store + 3 LOCAL aggregate counts into landing_page and render the front-door summary + 8-surface nav hub (THICK walking skeleton)
steps.description: The thick foundational walking-skeleton step (LD-WS, US-LD-001 Theme 1 — the riskiest-assumption thread). It forces the WHOLE landing-dashboard thread into existence end-to-end: viewer → 3 LOCAL aggregate reads → LandingSummary → pure render → full HTML page (summary + hub). (ports) Add `count_active_peer_subscriptions(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>` to StoreReadPort — a read-only sibling of the existing `count_claims`/`count_peer_claims` (ADR-054 D3); NO mutation method added to the port (ADR-030 / I-VIEW-1). (adapter-duckdb) Impl it as ONE aggregate `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM peer_subscriptions WHERE removed_at IS NULL` over the SAME shared connection (mirrors `count_claims` ~281), the SAME active-only definition as ADR-052's `list_active_peer_subscriptions` (a soft-removed row is residue, excluded; BR-LD-2); touches ONLY `peer_subscriptions` (no JOIN — `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` stays GREEN by construction). (viewer-domain) Add `pub struct LandingSummary { own_claims: Option<usize>, peer_claims: Option<usize>, active_peers: Option<usize> }` (ADR-054 D1 — three INDEPENDENT Option<usize>), `pub const MISSING_COUNT_MARKER = "—"`, and `pub const SCRAPE_URL = "/scrape"` (ADR-054 D4 — the 8th URL const, the one entry-point surface lacking one); extend `render_landing(&LandingSummary) -> String` (~554) from the slice-06 storeless shape (h1 + READ_ONLY_NOTICE + single /claims link) to render the 3 counts (Some(n) → the number; None → MISSING_COUNT_MARKER) + the 8-surface nav hub (plain `<a href>` via the URL consts MY_CLAIMS_URL/PEER_CLAIMS_URL/PROJECT_URL/PHILOSOPHY_URL/SCORE_URL/SEARCH_URL/SCRAPE_URL/PEERS_URL) + keep the `<h1>` + READ_ONLY_NOTICE. Full-page-only — `render_landing` returns a complete document, NO Shape fork (ADR-054 D5; parity by construction). The pure render is a TOTAL function of LandingSummary (every Option combination renders; no panic, no I/O). (adapter-http-viewer) Thread `store: &dyn StoreReadPort` into `landing_page` (~414, mirroring peers_page/claims_page), and update the route arm `"/" => Ok(landing_page(store.as_ref()))` (~354) to pass the store the ViewerServer ALREADY holds (NO new Option<Shared*> field); resolve the 3 counts INDEPENDENTLY via `.ok()` (Result→Option, the slice-12 graceful-degrade precedent ADR-048 generalized), build the LandingSummary, call `render_landing(&summary)` and wrap html_ok (always 200). (xtask) UNCHANGED — a read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge, so `check_viewer_capability_boundary` stays GREEN. Outcome: a seeded store (12 own, 7 peer, 2 active) + GET / returns a 200 full page showing "12 own claims, 7 peer claims, 2 active peers" AND nav links to all 8 surfaces (+ keeps h1 + READ_ONLY_NOTICE). BUILD-BEFORE-RUN (mirrors slice-06..16): the AT spawns the openlore (viewer) bin via ViewerServer::start, so the run MUST cargo build the bin before executing these ATs. NO second binary (the three reads are LOCAL DuckDB COUNT(*) aggregates). The seeds (seed_landing_store_summary) + asserts (assert_landing_shows_count, assert_landing_links_all_surfaces) already compile in support/mod.rs (they drive the EXISTING claim add/peer add/peer pull verbs + scan strings).
criteria: GET / over a REAL local store seeded (production `claim add` + `peer add` + `peer pull` paths, Pillar 3) with 12 own claims, 7 peer claims, and 2 active peer subscriptions returns a 200 full page (is_full_page()) showing the three counts (12 own claims, 7 peer claims, 2 active peers), the full 8-surface nav hub, and the preserved READ_ONLY_NOTICE.
StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method `count_active_peer_subscriptions(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>`; NO mutation method is added to the port (ADR-030 / I-VIEW-1).

DuckDbStoreReadAdapter impls it as ONE aggregate `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM peer_subscriptions WHERE removed_at IS NULL` over the shared connection (the active-only definition, BR-LD-2) — touching ONLY peer_subscriptions, no JOIN.

viewer-domain adds `LandingSummary` (3 × Option<usize>), `MISSING_COUNT_MARKER`, and `SCRAPE_URL` (the 8th URL const); `render_landing(&LandingSummary)` renders the 3 counts (Some(n) → number, None → marker) + the 8-surface plain-<a-href> hub + keeps h1 + READ_ONLY_NOTICE, full-page-only (no Shape fork, ADR-054 D5) as a TOTAL function of the summary.

adapter-http-viewer threads `store: &dyn StoreReadPort` into landing_page (no new Option<Shared*> field), resolves the 3 counts INDEPENDENTLY via `.ok()`, builds the LandingSummary, calls render_landing(&summary), and always returns 200; xtask check-arch stays GREEN (the read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge, check_viewer_capability_boundary unchanged).
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a REAL local store seeded (production `claim add` + `peer add` + `peer pull` paths, Pillar 3) with 12 own claims, 7 peer claims, and 2 active peer subscriptions returns a 200 full page (is_full_page()) showing the three counts (12 own claims, 7 peer claims, 2 active peers), the full 8-surface nav hub, and the preserved READ_ONLY_NOTICE.
StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method `count_active_peer_subscriptions(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>`; NO mutation method is added to the port (ADR-030 / I-VIEW-1).

DuckDbStoreReadAdapter impls it as ONE aggregate `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM peer_subscriptions WHERE removed_at IS NULL` over the shared connection (the active-only definition, BR-LD-2) — touching ONLY peer_subscriptions, no JOIN.

viewer-domain adds `LandingSummary` (3 × Option<usize>), `MISSING_COUNT_MARKER`, and `SCRAPE_URL` (the 8th URL const); `render_landing(&LandingSummary)` renders the 3 counts (Some(n) → number, None → marker) + the 8-surface plain-<a-href> hub + keeps h1 + READ_ONLY_NOTICE, full-page-only (no Shape fork, ADR-054 D5) as a TOTAL function of the summary.

adapter-http-viewer threads `store: &dyn StoreReadPort` into landing_page (no new Option<Shared*> field), resolves the 3 counts INDEPENDENTLY via `.ok()`, builds the LandingSummary, calls render_landing(&summary), and always returns 200; xtask check-arch stays GREEN (the read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge, check_viewer_capability_boundary unchanged).
steps.mutation_note: Adds the load-bearing pure-core surface (LandingSummary + the extended render_landing rendering 3 counts as Some(n)→number / None→MISSING_COUNT_MARKER + the 8-surface hub) and the count-only read seam (count_active_peer_subscriptions). Extend nightly mutation testing to the per-count render branch (Some(n) renders n, None renders the marker), the COUNT(*) WHERE removed_at IS NULL active-only filter, and the hub-link set (all 8 consts present).

steps.name: The hub links every shipped surface (8) as plain <a href> with no deep route, each via its URL constant (incl. the minted /scrape)
steps.description: Close the discoverability contract of US-LD-001 Theme 2 (LD-DISCOVER C-3 + LD-URLCONST). (LD-DISCOVER) The nav hub links ALL 8 shipped top-level surfaces — My Claims (/claims), Peer Claims (/peer-claims), Project Survey (/project), Philosophy Survey (/philosophy), Contributor Score (/score), Network Search (/search), Live Scrape (/scrape), Peer Subscriptions (/peers) — each as a plain `<a href>` (no-JS navigable), and NO deep/parameterized route (/claims/{cid}, /score?contributor, /project?subject) is a top-level hub link (FR-LD-5). Closes the discoverability gap (today only /claims is reachable from /). (LD-URLCONST) Each hub link's href equals the route's single-source-of-truth URL const value (MY_CLAIMS_URL, PEER_CLAIMS_URL, PROJECT_URL, PHILOSOPHY_URL, SCORE_URL, SEARCH_URL, PEERS_URL, and the NEW SCRAPE_URL = "/scrape") — no link is a hardcoded literal that could drift (R-LD-4). The minted SCRAPE_URL (01-01) closes the one missing const, making the hub 8 consistent consts. Direct confirmations of the 01-01 hub render; pin the hrefs (incl. the explicit href="/scrape") and the no-deep-route guard.
criteria: GET / over a seeded store renders the nav hub linking all 8 shipped top-level surfaces (/claims, /peer-claims, /project, /philosophy, /score, /search, /scrape, /peers), each a plain `<a href>` (assert_landing_links_all_surfaces).
NO deep/parameterized route (e.g. /claims/{cid}, /score?contributor, /project?subject) is a top-level hub link (assert_landing_no_deep_route_toplevel; FR-LD-5).

Each hub link's href equals its route's canonical URL-const value (no drifting literal, R-LD-4) — the 8 hrefs match the consts MY_CLAIMS_URL/PEER_CLAIMS_URL/PROJECT_URL/PHILOSOPHY_URL/SCORE_URL/SEARCH_URL/PEERS_URL + the minted SCRAPE_URL.
The newly-minted /scrape link is present (href="/scrape", ADR-054 D4) — the one const that did not exist before slice-17 — making every hub link a URL const.
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a seeded store renders the nav hub linking all 8 shipped top-level surfaces (/claims, /peer-claims, /project, /philosophy, /score, /search, /scrape, /peers), each a plain `<a href>` (assert_landing_links_all_surfaces).
NO deep/parameterized route (e.g. /claims/{cid}, /score?contributor, /project?subject) is a top-level hub link (assert_landing_no_deep_route_toplevel; FR-LD-5).

Each hub link's href equals its route's canonical URL-const value (no drifting literal, R-LD-4) — the 8 hrefs match the consts MY_CLAIMS_URL/PEER_CLAIMS_URL/PROJECT_URL/PHILOSOPHY_URL/SCORE_URL/SEARCH_URL/PEERS_URL + the minted SCRAPE_URL.
The newly-minted /scrape link is present (href="/scrape", ADR-054 D4) — the one const that did not exist before slice-17 — making every hub link a URL const.
steps.mutation_note: The 8-link hub (each href = its URL const, no deep route as a top-level link) is load-bearing for discoverability; mutation-test the hub-link set so a mutant that drops a surface, swaps a const for a drifting literal, or promotes a deep route to a top-level link is killed.

steps.name: A fresh empty store shows honest zero counts (0/0/0), DISTINCT from the missing-number marker, plus the full hub
steps.description: The honest-zeros edge (LD-ZEROS; US-LD-001 Theme 1 Ex 2 — the success side of `0 ≠ missing`). A fresh operator with 0 own claims, 0 peer claims, and 0 active subscriptions (seed_empty_store_for_landing — the three reads return Ok(0) → Some(0)) opens GET / and sees the summary showing 0 own claims, 0 peer claims, and 0 active peers — each a SUCCESSFUL read of zero (an honest empty store), NOT the missing-number marker — plus the full nav hub (so a brand-new operator can reach /scrape or /search to start). Confirmatory once 01-01 exists: the Some(0) → "0" render branch is exercised, and the assertion that the missing marker is ABSENT pins the success side of the type-level `0 ≠ missing` distinction (WD-LD-8; the failure side is pinned by 02-01 / 03-01).
criteria: GET / over a fresh initialized store with ZERO claims, peer claims, and subscriptions (seed_empty_store_for_landing) returns a 200 full page.
The summary shows honest zeros: 0 own claims, 0 peer claims, 0 active peers (each Some(0) → "0", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero).
The missing-number marker (MISSING_COUNT_MARKER "—") is ABSENT — an empty store is a successful read of zero, NOT a failed read (the success side of `0 ≠ missing`, WD-LD-8).
The full 8-surface nav hub still renders so a brand-new operator can navigate (assert_landing_links_all_surfaces).
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a fresh initialized store with ZERO claims, peer claims, and subscriptions (seed_empty_store_for_landing) returns a 200 full page.
The summary shows honest zeros: 0 own claims, 0 peer claims, 0 active peers (each Some(0) → "0", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero).
The missing-number marker (MISSING_COUNT_MARKER "—") is ABSENT — an empty store is a successful read of zero, NOT a failed read (the success side of `0 ≠ missing`, WD-LD-8).
The full 8-surface nav hub still renders so a brand-new operator can navigate (assert_landing_links_all_surfaces).
steps.mutation_note: The Some(0) → "0" (not the missing marker) render branch is the success side of `0 ≠ missing`; mutation-test it so a mutant that renders Some(0) as the missing marker (or blanks the zero) is killed.

steps.name: The front door exposes no write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — every affordance is a plain link (CARDINAL read-only)
steps.description: The read-only / no-write story scenario (LD-READONLY; US-LD-001 Theme 3 / C-1 CARDINAL). When Maria inspects the rendered GET / page over a seeded store, it contains no `<form>`, no `<button>`, and no control to compose/sign/subscribe/follow — every navigation affordance is a plain `<a href>` link, not a mutating control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control). The no-key guarantee is structural (the slice-06 web_process_holds_no_signing_key gold + xtask check-arch); here the operator-facing surface carries no mutating control. Confirmatory once the hub render (01-01/01-02) exists — the 8 hub links are plain links by construction; this step pins that NO mutating control crept into the new richer page. Mirrors the slice-15 PS-INV-NoWrite story-level guardrail.
criteria: GET / over a seeded store returns 200 so the no-control scan is over REAL rendered content (not an error/empty page).
The page contains no `<form>`, no `<button>`, and no compose/sign/subscribe/follow control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control) — C-1 CARDINAL.
Every navigation affordance on the front door is a plain `<a href>` link, never a mutating control.
No write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow affordance is introduced by the new summary + hub render (the slice-06 no-key guarantee is inherited; the new page adds no write surface).
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a seeded store returns 200 so the no-control scan is over REAL rendered content (not an error/empty page).
The page contains no `<form>`, no `<button>`, and no compose/sign/subscribe/follow control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control) — C-1 CARDINAL.
Every navigation affordance on the front door is a plain `<a href>` link, never a mutating control.
No write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow affordance is introduced by the new summary + hub render (the slice-06 no-key guarantee is inherited; the new page adds no write surface).
steps.mutation_note: The render-only-ness of the front door is CARDINAL; mutation-test the render so a mutant that wraps a hub link in a <form>/<button> or emits a compose/sign/subscribe/follow control is killed.

phases.name: Front-door edges — missing≠zero degrade (new fault seam + xtask guard), offline, aggregate-not-merge, active-only

steps.name: A failed peer-claims-count read degrades to the missing-number state "—" without a 5xx (the genuinely-new fault seam + xtask release-gate guard)
steps.description: The one step carrying genuinely-NEW fault-injection work (LD-DEGRADE; US-LD-000/001 Theme 4 / C-2 CARDINAL — the per-count independent degrade). Maria's peer-claims-count read fails transiently while the own-claims and active-peer reads succeed (12 own, 2 active). When she opens GET /, the nav hub renders in full, own-claims shows 12 and active-peers shows 2, the peer-claims number renders as the missing-number marker "—" (MISSING_COUNT_MARKER, NOT a fabricated 0), and the page is a normal 200 — never a 5xx, never a raw stack trace. The per-count independent degrade: each count is resolved by its own `.ok()` (Result → Option), so a failed `count_peer_claims` maps to None → marker while the other two stay Some(_) → number (ADR-054 D2; the slice-12 ADR-048 unwrap_or_default precedent generalized). DELIVER materializes the test-only effect-shell fault seam `start_viewer_with_failing_peer_claims_count` → the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT` env var, MIRRORING slice-16's `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_ACTIVE_SET_READ` (adapter-http-viewer ~1064): a `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-gated `peer_claims_count_with_fault_seam(read)` that substitutes `Err(StoreReadError::Unreadable)` when the env var is set so the SAME production `.ok()` degrade branch runs, plus the `#[cfg(not(debug_assertions))]` release identity (NO env-var read compiled in). The DISTILL harness already references `start_viewer_with_failing_peer_claims_count` + the start_inner param (a todo!() RED scaffold until DELIVER materializes it). (xtask) Add the NEW release-gate guard for this seam: a `scan_viewer_fail_seam_guard`-style scan for the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT` token (check_arch.rs ~1029-1049, mirroring `VIEWER_FAIL_ACTIVE_SET_READ_SEAM_TOKEN`) so an UNGATED read in a release build fails check-arch — extend the guard to cover BOTH tokens (the slice-16 one stays, the slice-17 one is added). This pins Earned Trust on the store-read dependency: the design exercises the substrate LYING (a transient StoreReadError) and proves the front door survives it without 5xx and without fabricating 0.
criteria: GET / against a viewer whose peer-claims count read is forced to FAIL mid-request (start_viewer_with_failing_peer_claims_count → OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT; own + active reads still succeed) returns a NORMAL 200 — never a 5xx (C-2 CARDINAL / NFR-VIEW-6).
The nav hub still renders in full (the failure is per-count, not page-wide), and the two SUCCESSFUL counts still show their numbers (own claims 12, active peers 2).

The FAILED peer-claims count renders the missing-number marker "—" (MISSING_COUNT_MARKER, via None → marker), NOT a fabricated 0; no raw stack trace ("panicked at" / "RUST_BACKTRACE" / "stack backtrace") leaks to the rendered surface.

DELIVER materializes the test-only `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT` seam behind `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` (release identity is a no-op, mirroring slice-16's OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_ACTIVE_SET_READ), and the xtask release-gate guard is extended to scan for the new token (an UNGATED read fails check-arch); the per-count `.ok()` degrade is the production path under test.
acceptance_criteria: GET / against a viewer whose peer-claims count read is forced to FAIL mid-request (start_viewer_with_failing_peer_claims_count → OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT; own + active reads still succeed) returns a NORMAL 200 — never a 5xx (C-2 CARDINAL / NFR-VIEW-6).
The nav hub still renders in full (the failure is per-count, not page-wide), and the two SUCCESSFUL counts still show their numbers (own claims 12, active peers 2).

The FAILED peer-claims count renders the missing-number marker "—" (MISSING_COUNT_MARKER, via None → marker), NOT a fabricated 0; no raw stack trace ("panicked at" / "RUST_BACKTRACE" / "stack backtrace") leaks to the rendered surface.

DELIVER materializes the test-only `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT` seam behind `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` (release identity is a no-op, mirroring slice-16's OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_ACTIVE_SET_READ), and the xtask release-gate guard is extended to scan for the new token (an UNGATED read fails check-arch); the per-count `.ok()` degrade is the production path under test.
steps.mutation_note: The per-count `.ok()` degrade (failed peer-claims read → None → MISSING_COUNT_MARKER while the other two stay Some(_) → number, never a 5xx, never a fabricated 0) is load-bearing no-crash logic; mutation-test it so a mutant that fabricates 0 on failure, blanks the other counts, or propagates a 5xx is killed. The `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-gated seam + xtask guard are the release-safety enforcement (the seam never ships).

steps.name: The front door renders fully offline referencing only the vendored htmx asset, and the counts are aggregates never a merged consensus record
steps.description: Two front-door edges that flip green for free once 01-01 exists (LD-OFFLINE; US-LD-000/001 Theme 5 / C-2 + LD-AGGREGATE; US-LD-001 Theme 7 / C-7 / BR-LD-1). (LD-OFFLINE) With the store seeded and NO outbound network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start), GET / renders the store summary + the full nav hub fully offline — the three reads are LOCAL COUNT(*) aggregates with NO outbound edge (no PDS fetch, no DID re-resolution, no peer pull, no network search — offline-STRONGER than /search/scrape), and the page references ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() is false). (LD-AGGREGATE) The summary shows a SINGLE peer-claims count (an aggregate — how many), and renders NO per-author content/score/merged "consensus" row on the front door: the per-author DIDs (LANDING_PEER_RACHEL_DID, LANDING_PEER_TOBIAS_DID) must NOT appear on / (they are reached THROUGH /peer-claims, /peers, /score), and no merged-consensus phrasing ("consensus", "authors agree", "the network says") appears. The anti-merging invariant protects per-author CONTENT rendering; a store-wide count is a legitimate aggregate (WD-LD-6 / BR-LD-1).
criteria: GET / over a seeded store with NO outbound network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start) returns 200 and renders the full summary (12 own, 7 peer, 2 active) + the full 8-surface hub fully offline — the three LOCAL reads have no outbound edge to take down.
The page references ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js — references_external_cdn() is false (no off-host CDN; I-VIEW-6 / KPI-HX-G2).
The summary shows the SINGLE aggregate peer-claims count (7) — an aggregate, not a per-author breakdown — and the front door renders NO per-author content/score/merged-consensus row.

The per-author peer DIDs (LANDING_PEER_RACHEL_DID, LANDING_PEER_TOBIAS_DID) do NOT appear on / (content stays on the attributed surfaces), and no merged-consensus phrasing ("consensus", "authors agree", "the network says") appears (BR-LD-1).
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a seeded store with NO outbound network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start) returns 200 and renders the full summary (12 own, 7 peer, 2 active) + the full 8-surface hub fully offline — the three LOCAL reads have no outbound edge to take down.
The page references ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js — references_external_cdn() is false (no off-host CDN; I-VIEW-6 / KPI-HX-G2).
The summary shows the SINGLE aggregate peer-claims count (7) — an aggregate, not a per-author breakdown — and the front door renders NO per-author content/score/merged-consensus row.

The per-author peer DIDs (LANDING_PEER_RACHEL_DID, LANDING_PEER_TOBIAS_DID) do NOT appear on / (content stays on the attributed surfaces), and no merged-consensus phrasing ("consensus", "authors agree", "the network says") appears (BR-LD-1).
steps.mutation_note: The offline (no outbound edge / vendored-only htmx) + aggregate-not-merge (single peer-claims count, no per-author DID, no consensus phrasing) renders are load-bearing; mutation-test the no-CDN chrome reference and the aggregate-only render so a mutant that adds a CDN reference, emits a per-author DID, or renders a merged-consensus row is killed.

steps.name: A soft-removed peer is not counted in the active-peer summary (the active-only removed_at IS NULL definition)
steps.description: The active-only boundary (LD-SOFTREMOVED; US-LD-000 Theme 8 / BR-LD-2). Maria subscribed to did:plc:rachel-test then ran `openlore peer remove did:plc:rachel-test` (no --purge, via seed_peer_subscribed_then_removed — removed_at set, cached claims survive), and she still actively follows did:plc:tobias-test (a fresh active subscription via seed_active_subscription_for). When she opens GET /, the active-peer count is 1 (only the active Tobias subscription) and the soft-removed Rachel is NOT counted, and her DID does not appear on the front door (aggregate-only, Theme 7). This pins the active-only definition of count_active_peer_subscriptions (COUNT(*) WHERE removed_at IS NULL, ADR-054 D3 / BR-LD-2) on the rendered surface — the residue of a soft-removed peer is excluded from the front-door count even though its cached claims remain on disk. Confirmatory once the 01-01 count read + the 02-02 aggregate-only render exist; pins the residue-excluded property end-to-end.
criteria: GET / after Rachel was soft-removed via the real `openlore peer remove` verb (removed_at set, cached claims survive, no --purge — seed_peer_subscribed_then_removed) while Tobias stays an active subscription returns 200.
The active-peer count is 1 (only the active Tobias subscription — the active-only `removed_at IS NULL` set, BR-LD-2); the soft-removed Rachel is NOT counted.
The soft-removed Rachel's DID (LANDING_PEER_RACHEL_DID) does NOT appear on the front door (excluded from the active-peer count by the removed_at IS NULL filter; aggregate-only, Theme 7).

The count_active_peer_subscriptions COUNT(*) WHERE removed_at IS NULL active-only definition holds on the OBSERVABLE render — a soft-removed row is residue, never counted, even though its cached peer_claims remain on disk.
acceptance_criteria: GET / after Rachel was soft-removed via the real `openlore peer remove` verb (removed_at set, cached claims survive, no --purge — seed_peer_subscribed_then_removed) while Tobias stays an active subscription returns 200.
The active-peer count is 1 (only the active Tobias subscription — the active-only `removed_at IS NULL` set, BR-LD-2); the soft-removed Rachel is NOT counted.
The soft-removed Rachel's DID (LANDING_PEER_RACHEL_DID) does NOT appear on the front door (excluded from the active-peer count by the removed_at IS NULL filter; aggregate-only, Theme 7).

The count_active_peer_subscriptions COUNT(*) WHERE removed_at IS NULL active-only definition holds on the OBSERVABLE render — a soft-removed row is residue, never counted, even though its cached peer_claims remain on disk.
steps.mutation_note: The active-only filter (COUNT(*) WHERE removed_at IS NULL) is the CARDINAL residue boundary for the active-peer count; mutation-test it so a mutant that drops the filter (counting a soft-removed peer) is killed — the soft-removed residue must never inflate the front-door active-peer count.

phases.name: Gold guardrail invariants (front door — read-only, no-write, offline, no-N+1, missing≠zero, discoverability)

steps.name: Gold: the seven front-door guardrail invariants (read-only, no-write, offline-chrome, offline, no-N+1, missing≠zero, discoverability completeness)
steps.description: The seven cross-cutting GOLD guardrails (LD-INV-*) that must hold over the WHOLE GET / front-door surface — the BEHAVIORAL layer of the three-layer ADR-054 enforcement (type [StoreReadPort declares no mutation method] + xtask check_viewer_capability_boundary are the other two). They drive the REAL openlore ui subprocess + HTTP over a REAL seeded LOCAL DuckDB, no mocked boundary (/ is a LOCAL read + PURE render). (LD-INV-ReadOnly, C-1 / WD-LD-1) exercising GET / over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (universe-bound assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe; the LandingSummary is computed per request and persists nothing, I-LD-6). (LD-INV-NoWrite, C-1 CARDINAL) no GET / response renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — every affordance a plain <a href> (assert_landing_read_only_no_control). (LD-INV-OfflineChrome, C-2 / KPI-HX-G2) the page references ONLY the local /static/htmx.min.js, NO off-host CDN (references_external_cdn() false). (LD-INV-Offline, C-2 / KPI-5) the summary + full hub (the DATA, not just chrome) render fully network-down — offline-STRONGER (no outbound edge). (LD-INV-NoNPlus1, C-4 / I-LD-7) a LARGE store (120 own claims via seed_own_claims_via_cli + 2 active peers) renders the correct counts in ONE request — the read count is a FIXED set of 3 aggregate reads invariant to store size (the strict 3-fixed-reads bound is the DELIVER adapter-duckdb unit/property test). (LD-INV-MissingNotZero, C-2 / BR-LD-3 CARDINAL) BOTH sides: an empty store renders "0" for peer claims (NOT the marker), while a failed peer-claims read (via the 02-01 fault seam) renders the missing marker "—" (NOT a fabricated 0) — a fabricated 0 on failure is unrepresentable (the shell maps a failed read to None, never Some(0)). (LD-INV-Discoverability, C-3 / WD-LD-7) the hub links ALL 8 shipped top-level surfaces + no deep route as a top-level link (a missing surface is an unshippable navigation gap). These INHERIT the slice-06 viewer golds (viewer_is_read_only / store_views_work_offline / web_process_holds_no_signing_key) and add the FRONT-DOOR specifics. Flip green for free once 01-01..02-03 exist (the MissingNotZero failure side reuses the 02-01 fault seam). LAST roadmap step.
criteria: LD-INV-ReadOnly: exercising GET / over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe, all slots unchanged) — the LandingSummary persists nothing (I-LD-6 / WD-LD-1).
LD-INV-NoWrite (CARDINAL): no GET / response renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — every affordance a plain <a href> (assert_landing_read_only_no_control); the viewer holds no key.

LD-INV-OfflineChrome + LD-INV-Offline: the page references ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false), AND the summary + full hub (the DATA) render fully network-down with NO outbound edge (offline-STRONGER than /search).

LD-INV-NoNPlus1 + LD-INV-MissingNotZero + LD-INV-Discoverability: a LARGE store (120 own claims + 2 active peers) renders the correct counts in ONE request (3 fixed aggregate reads, invariant to size); the empty store renders "0" for peer claims (NOT the marker) while the failed-read path (02-01 seam) renders the marker "—" (NOT a fabricated 0) — both sides of `0 ≠ missing`; and the hub links ALL 8 surfaces with no deep route as a top-level link.

LD-INV-NoWrite (CARDINAL): no GET / response renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — every affordance a plain <a href> (assert_landing_read_only_no_control); the viewer holds no key.

LD-INV-OfflineChrome + LD-INV-Offline: the page references ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false), AND the summary + full hub (the DATA) render fully network-down with NO outbound edge (offline-STRONGER than /search).

LD-INV-NoNPlus1 + LD-INV-MissingNotZero + LD-INV-Discoverability: a LARGE store (120 own claims + 2 active peers) renders the correct counts in ONE request (3 fixed aggregate reads, invariant to size); the empty store renders "0" for peer claims (NOT the marker) while the failed-read path (02-01 seam) renders the marker "—" (NOT a fabricated 0) — both sides of `0 ≠ missing`; and the hub links ALL 8 surfaces with no deep route as a top-level link.
steps.mutation_note: These seven guardrails are the behavioral layer of the ADR-054 three-layer enforcement; ensure mutation testing of the pure render core (per-count Some(n)→number / None→marker, the 8-link hub, no-control, no-CDN) and the per-count `.ok()` degrade is green so the gold guardrails are not the only line of defense. The no-N+1 (3 fixed aggregate reads invariant to store size) + missing≠zero (both sides) + discoverability (all 8 surfaces) are the front-door-specific additions over the inherited slice-06 golds.
